channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 2 hours.

item.title: 📍 The United States-Cuba Oil Embargo and International Law | Just Security
item.description: Experts on naval warfare examine the international law dimensions of the United States campaign against Cuba. The post The United States-Cuba Oil Embargo and International Law appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🏛️ USS Wasp sees five-year service life extension | Breaking Defense
item.description: “The other LHDs — we’ve got to study to see if we can extend them, and the plan is going to be to do that,” a top Marine said.

item.title: 📍 The Governance Gap Mythos Exposed—And How to Address It | Just Security
item.description: When the consequences of one corporate decision can compromise the world’s digital infrastructure, industry self-governance is not enough. The post The Governance Gap Mythos Exposed—And How to Address It appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🏴 War Is Killing Ukraine’s Oligarchy | Foreign Policy
item.description: The fight against Russia is accomplishing what years of struggle against corruption couldn’t.

item.title: 🌍 Early Edition: April 29, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated weekday guide to major news and developments over the last 24 hours. Here’s today’s news: IRAN WAR – CEASEFIRE President Trump has instructed aides to prepare for an extended blockade of Iran as negotiations…

item.title: 🌍 Charai for The Jerusalem Strategic Tribune: The Crown’s Moral Voice: King Charles in Washington and the Test of Western Clarity | Atlantic Council
item.description: The post Charai for The Jerusalem Strategic Tribune: The Crown’s Moral Voice: King Charles in Washington and the Test of Western Clarity appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 Hedging Is the New Normal | Foreign Policy
item.description: For the foreseeable future, successful statecraft will depend on hedging.

item.title: 🏴 The EU Is the New Go-To Middle Power | Foreign Policy
item.description: In a world of disorder, the bloc’s boring stability is suddenly attractive.

item.title: 🌏 The Taiwan Evacuation Trap | Foreign Policy
item.description: Washington has no good options for evacuating Americans in a crisis.

item.title: 📍 German Foreign Policy Has Finally Grown Up | Foreign Policy
item.description: Friedrich Merz is, slowly but surely, putting his country on a new international footing.

item.title: 📍 Aligning the U.S. and Canadian Defense Industrial Bases | War on the Rocks
item.description: The United States and Canada are both racing to rebuild their defense industrial bases, recognizing that future conflicts will be determined not only by military capability, but by the ability to produce at scale. But they cannot succeed alone — and importantly, they do not need…

item.title: 🛡️ Western Withdrawal, Jihadist Expansion: How the Sahel Became Ground Zero for Global Terrorism | War on the Rocks
item.description: On April 25, armed groups launched near-simultaneous attacks against military installations and key strategic sites across Mali. Claimed by Jama’at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin, a jihadist group, and conducted in coordination with Tuareg separatist forces from the Front de…

item.title: 🌏 Warning signs: how China normalises its presence | ASPI Strategist
item.description: A major strategic shift in the Indo-Pacific is happening quietly: China’s presence in seas and ports far from its coast is becoming routine. The frequency of its appearances has been rising for about a decade. ...

item.title: 📍 Why Indonesia Floated a Malacca Toll | Foreign Policy
item.description: An apparently off-the-cuff observation sparked consternation across the region.

item.title: 🌏 The Real Threat to Taiwan | Foreign Affairs
item.description: America Is preparing for the wrong kind of crisis

item.title: 🏴 The Transatlantic MAGA Fantasy | Foreign Affairs
item.description: How Trump misread Europe.

item.title: 🌍 How the War Saved the Iranian Regime | Foreign Affairs
item.description: The unintended consequences of the U.S.-Israeli assault.

item.title: 🌏 Interpreting the Future of Vietnam-China Relations Through the 2026 Joint Statement | The Diplomat
item.description: The recent visit to China of Vietnam's top leader To Lam could well mark a turning point in the sometimes fraught bilateral relationship.

item.title: 🌏 Creating a northern hybrid zone to turn ambition into action | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Washington and Manila have moved from intent to design, announcing plans for a 4,000-acre (16 square km) economic security zone to anchor allied supply chains in the Philippines. That move should inspire Australia’s approach to ...

item.title: 🌍 Israel and Lebanon are closer to peace than they appear | Atlantic Council
item.description: But the two sides still need to arrive at a face-saving compromise on Hezbollah’s disarmament and Israel’s buffer zone. The post Israel and Lebanon are closer to peace than they appear appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏛️ Acting SecNav: ‘I’m not going to have my son go to war the way I did' | Defense One
item.description: Industry can help prevent the Pentagon from making GWOT-era mistakes, said Hung Cao, filling in for his recently ousted boss.

item.title: 🌍 ‘A long time coming’: How to understand the UAE’s decision to leave OPEC | Atlantic Council
item.description: It’s important to see Abu Dhabi's announcement in the context of the growing rivalry between the UAE and Saudi Arabia. The post ‘A long time coming’: How to understand the UAE’s decision to leave OPEC appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 SOCOM adding AI, autonomy ‘at every level,’ commander says | Defense One
item.description: Fast adoption illustrates smaller organizations’ ability to harness disruptive tech.

item.title: 🏛️ ‘Alpha’ troops and more ships: Acting Navy Secretary Hung Cao outlines vision for service | Defense News
item.description: In one of his first public addresses as acting Navy secretary, Hung Cao said he didn't need "cross-dressers" or "leaf eaters" in the service.

item.title: 📍 King Charles III: ‘A Partnership Born Out of Dispute’ | Foreign Policy
item.description: The British monarch praises multilateral institutions in a pointed message to the Trump administration.

item.title: 🏛️ King Charles III Delivers Pointed Message in Address to U.S. Congress | Foreign Policy
item.description: The British monarch praised multilateral institutions, warned against climate change, and championed diversity.

item.title: 📍 Why is the UAE leaving OPEC? | Atlantic Council
item.description: Our experts unpack the geopolitical and energy market impacts of the UAE's just-announced departure. The post Why is the UAE leaving OPEC? appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: ⚛️ New Cruise Missile-Armed MV-75 Tiltrotor Concept For The Marines Shown Off | The War Zone
item.description: The Marines have said "everything is on the table" when it comes to potential replacements for their AH-1 and UH-1 helicopters. The post New Cruise Missile-Armed MV-75 Tiltrotor Concept For The Marines Shown Off appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ Cao says industry needs to ‘move forward’ in first speech as acting SecNav | Breaking Defense
item.description: Acting Secretary of the Navy Hung Cao delivered his first public remarks since stepping into the role.

item.title: 🌏 China Pulls the Plug on Meta’s AI Acquisition | Foreign Policy
item.description: The reversal underscores Beijing’s shifting national security concerns.
